common.disclaimer: Not a diagnosis · Does not replace a professional.

instrumento.help: If you don't know an answer, leave it blank or move on to the next one. You can change an answer by tapping another option.

promotor.title: Your health worker space
promotor.subtitle: Your people and what's best to do today.
promotor.paraHoy: For today · who to visit or call
promotor.empty: All up to date. When you screen someone, their follow-up will show up here.

inicio.title: What brings you here today?
inicio.subtitle: Brain health close to you, before and after a diagnosis. Start with what you need.
inicio.invitedBanner: You were invited to support someone. Enter through «I care for someone».

persona.t: I want to look after my memory
persona.d: A brief, clear assessment, and what to do next.

cuidador.d: Look after yourself, handle difficult situations, and assess the person you care for.

promotor.d: Your people, who to visit, and field screening.

inicio.pro: I'm a professional at the Memory Unit
inicio.privacy: Your data stays on this device. You decide what to share.

home.demoBanner: Network tour · demonstration view: enter each space to see how it connects.
home.title: Choose your space
home.subtitle: This network supports brain health before and after diagnosis. Enter through the space that represents you.
home.privacy: Your data stays on this device. You decide what to share.

ejemplo.banner: Example case · demonstration data (not a real person).

paciente.title: Person
paciente.desc: Self-assessment and education in plain language.

cuidador.title: Caregiver
cuidador.desc: Support, symptom management, and looking after yourself.

unidad.title: Professional / Unit
unidad.desc: Pre-consultation inbox with summary and priority.

comunidad.title: Community
comunidad.desc: Network governance: what we ask for and what we change.

dashboard.phaseNote: Space under construction — co-designed with the community.

0.d: A brief screening in plain language.

1.d: What it means and what to do now.

2.d: A step-by-step guide to living better.

1.t: What to do in each situation

4.t: Screen the person I care for
4.d: Assess your family member based on what you notice.

0.d: People being followed up in the community.

1.d: Modifiable risk, MRCA and flagged medication.

2.d: People, caregivers and agents who co-decide.

3.d: How the data is cared for and whose it is.

steps.ad8: Have you noticed changes in your memory?

microVictoria.vos: Done! We now know the basics about you.
microVictoria.salud: Great! You finished the part about your health.
microVictoria.entorno: Your home and surroundings are done!

preconsulta.opcionalSumar: The essentials are done — you can add more (optional)

modo.title: Who is filling this out?
modo.intro: This adapts the questions: the person self-assesses; the relative/caregiver answers as an informant.
modo.agente: I am a health agent or promoter

prevencion.title: Prevention — factors you can take care of
prevencion.intro: Answer honestly. This is an estimate, not a diagnosis: it shows you which factors you can work on to care for your brain health.

factores.title: A few more questions about your habits
factores.intro: These help us better understand your brain health. Whatever you don't know, leave blank.

demografia.title: About you
demografia.intro: This information helps us better interpret your result.
demografia.fieldHint: Fields with * are required. If you don't know something, leave it blank.
demografia.aliasHint: No full name. It helps us recognize your follow-up without exposing your identity.
demografia.eduHint: How many years you attended school (primary, secondary, etc.).
demografia.vive_campo: In the countryside / a rural area
demografia.cercaLabel: How far away is the nearest health center?
demografia.cerca_nose: I don't know / there isn't one

mrca.title: Memory and cognitive changes
mrca.intro: Think about the last few months and answer Yes or No. These answers feed the model.
mrca.note: These answers feed the Kaizen-MRCA model (first-level estimate, not a diagnosis).
mrca.modelNoteLay: First-level estimate for guidance, not a diagnosis.
mrca.modelNote: Estimate from the Kaizen-MRCA model (AUC ~0.80 in development / ~0.63 in validation). First-level screening, not a diagnosis.

items.recent: Do you forget recent conversations or things more than before?
items.repeat: Do you repeat questions or comments without realizing it?
items.words: Do you struggle to find words you know?
items.orientation: Do you get disoriented or lost in familiar places?
items.help: Do you need more help than before with tasks like accounts or medication?
items.slow: Do you feel that you think or react more slowly?
items.informant: Has someone close to you mentioned that your memory has changed?

medicacion.title: Medication
medicacion.intro: Add the medications you take. I'll flag what's worth reviewing (nothing is prescribed).
medicacion.empty: You haven't added any medications yet.

flags.poly: You take {{n}} medications (5 or more). It's worth reviewing with your team whether they're all necessary.
flags.acb: Some of your medications may affect memory. It's worth reviewing them with your doctor.
flags.bzd: {{n}} medication(s) for sleep or to calm the nerves: they can cause falls or confusion. Worth reviewing.
flags.beers: {{n}} medication(s) that in older adults are worth reviewing with the health team.
flags.antidementia: There is already ongoing memory treatment (donepezil/memantine) — informational.

medicacion.disclaimer: Flags for professional review. Does not prescribe or discontinue treatments. Indicative values.

banderas.title: Warning signs
banderas.intro: Check if any of these situations appear.
banderas.help: Only check the ones you noticed. If you're not sure, leave it unchecked.
banderas.warning: There are warning signs: a priority consultation is advisable.

items.focal: Weakness, difficulty speaking or seeing on one side.
items.earlyOnset: Symptoms in a young person or of unusual onset.
items.behavior: Strong behavioral changes or risky situations.

education.label: Education
education.question: Did you have few educational opportunities in childhood (you didn't finish primary school)?
education.advice: Keep your mind active: read, learn new things, play memory games. It's never too late.

hearing.label: Hearing
hearing.question: Do you have trouble hearing, or should you be using a hearing aid?
hearing.advice: Get your hearing checked. Using a hearing aid when needed protects your memory.

ldl.label: Cholesterol (LDL)
ldl.question: Do you have high “bad” cholesterol (LDL)?
ldl.advice: Manage your cholesterol with your health team: diet, activity and, if needed, medication.

depression.label: Mood
depression.question: Do you feel sad, low, or uninterested most of the time?
depression.advice: Asking for help works: depression is treatable. Talk to your health team.

tbi.label: Head injuries
tbi.question: Have you had serious head injuries with loss of consciousness?
tbi.advice: Protect your head: wear a helmet on a motorcycle or bike, and watch out for falls at home.

inactivity.label: Physical activity
inactivity.question: Do you get little physical activity (you hardly walk or move around)?
inactivity.advice: Moving helps your brain. Aim for about 30 minutes of walking most days.

diabetes.label: Diabetes
diabetes.question: Do you have diabetes or high blood sugar?
diabetes.advice: Manage your blood sugar with your health team: you protect your brain and your heart.

smoking.label: Tobacco
smoking.question: Do you smoke?
smoking.advice: Quitting smoking lowers your risk at any age. Ask for help to do it.

hypertension.label: Blood pressure
hypertension.question: Do you have high blood pressure (hypertension)?
hypertension.advice: Keep your blood pressure in check. Keeping it in a healthy range protects your memory.

obesity.label: Weight
obesity.question: Are you overweight or living with obesity?
obesity.advice: Small changes in food and movement help. Look for support, without guilt.

alcohol.label: Alcohol
alcohol.question: Do you drink alcohol in excess (more than a few drinks a week)?
alcohol.advice: Less alcohol is better for your brain. If it's hard, ask for help.

isolation.label: Social connections
isolation.question: Do you spend most of your time alone or with little contact with others?
isolation.advice: Nurture your connections: staying in touch with other people cares for your mind.

airPollution.label: Air and smoke
airPollution.question: Are you heavily exposed to smoke or polluted air (wood-fire cooking, tobacco smoke)?
airPollution.advice: Ventilate your rooms, avoid smoke, and improve your stove if you cook with wood.

vision.label: Vision
vision.question: Do you have uncorrected vision problems (you don't wear the glasses you need)?
vision.advice: Get your eyes checked. Correcting your vision protects your memory and your independence.

invite.title: Invite a family member
invite.desc: Sharing care works: a family member can support you and learn how to help. It's what most sustains follow-up.
invite.msg: Hi, I'm taking care of my brain health with KaizenAI. Will you join me? Come in here:

meaning.verde: Green — take it easy. We didn't see any signs that call for an urgent consultation. Keep up with your usual check-ups and take care of your habits.
meaning.amarillo: Yellow — worth checking, no need to worry. It's not urgent, but it would be good to have a professional evaluate you in the coming weeks.
meaning.rojo: Red — better not to wait. A specialist should see you soon. This does NOT mean you have a disease: it's so you get seen quickly.

triage.instrumentsSkipped: If you left any section unanswered, it doesn't appear here. Completing them gives your team more detail.
triage.comparadoresIntro: These are scientific risk rules. They look at similar things from different angles. What matters is the trend, not the exact number.

urgent.title: Signs it's best not to wait on

action.verde: Low risk: prevention and follow-up in the community. Keep taking care of the modifiable factors.
action.amarillo: A scheduled evaluation at the Memory Unit is advisable. In the meantime, work on the modifiable factors.
action.rojo: A priority referral to the Memory Unit is suggested. See someone soon.

reasons.mrcaAlto: The MRCA model estimated high risk.
reasons.mrcaModerado: The MRCA model estimated moderate risk.
reasons.mrcaDerivar: The MRCA model suggests referral (uncertain to rule out).
reasons.equidad: Context of greater vulnerability (equity): we prioritize not losing follow-up.
reasons.low: No warning signs or high scores.

stats.riskHelp: Health habits and care that are in your hands. Several showing up isn't bad news: they're exactly the things you can act on.
stats.mrcaHelp: An estimate based on your answers, not a diagnosis. Low = keep going as you are · Moderate = worth checking without rushing · High = seek an appointment soon.

triage.factorsExplain: We found {{n}} of 14 things that are in your hands, like {{ejemplos}}. Several showing up is good news: they're exactly the ones you can work on to take care of your memory.
triage.factorsExplainNone: You didn't mark any factors to improve among the 14 we looked at. Keep taking care of your habits.

mrcaBandExplain.bajo: Based on your answers, not many signs appear. Keep going as you are.
mrcaBandExplain.moderado: It's not an emergency, but it's worth having a professional review it for your peace of mind.
mrcaBandExplain.alto: It's worth seeking an evaluation soon. This is an estimate, not a diagnosis.

export.title: Share with the health team
export.privacyNote: The alias travels in the WhatsApp link unencrypted. If you prefer more privacy, use “Copy summary”.

triage.disclaimerShort: Guidance estimate. Not a diagnosis — check with your health team.
triage.disclaimer: Guidance result (estimate with values to be validated). It does not diagnose or replace the professional. Your data stays on this device.

ficha.noEncontrado: We couldn't find this person's report.

aviso.title: Legal notice and privacy
aviso.intro: How your data is handled in KaizenAI. This app does not diagnose and does not replace a professional.

baseLegal.d: Law 25.326 on the Protection of Personal Data (Argentina). Health data is a sensitive category and requires consent.

datos.d: Brain health data (risk factors, mood, self-reported memory, medication) and contact details. Only the minimum necessary is requested.

derechos.d: You can access, correct and delete your data (habeas data) from 'My data': view, export or delete.

onDevice.d: On this device (local-first). There is no central server in this version; you control what you share.

noDx.d: The results are indicative estimates to support the consultation, not a diagnosis or a medical device.

aviso.nota: Demonstration version. The data controller and the final policy will be completed for the pilot.

perfil.title: Brain health profile map
perfil.intro: What a person's profile can cover, organized by domain. You don't need to complete it all: it fills in gradually and among several people.
perfil.nota: Structure based on the WHO brain health domains. The completeness meter appears during the check-up.

ipaq.title: Physical activity (past week)
ipaq.intro: Tell us how many days per week and minutes per day you did each type of activity.
ipaq.vigorosa: Intense activity (running, carrying weight, hard sport)
ipaq.moderada: Moderate activity (easy cycling, tasks that tire you a little)
ipaq.caminar: Walking (at least 10 minutes at a time)
ipaq.sentado: Time spent sitting on a typical day

pro.title: For the professional · model and comparators
pro.mrcaValidation: AUC ~0.80 in development / ~0.63 in validation. First-level screening, not a diagnosis.
pro.lancetNote: The Lancet Commission's "modifiable risk" is a POPULATION metric (attributable fraction, PAF), not an individual risk. That is why the factors present are shown here, not an individual percentage. Individual risk is given by the MRCA model and the comparators below.
pro.contribsNote: Contribution of each factor to the estimated ACE-III (exact SHAP of the linear model). Negative values lower the estimated ACE (worse performance).
pro.disclaimer: Research comparators, weighted by relative risk; they require LOCAL RECALIBRATION. Not for clinical decisions in isolation.

equity.title: Support context
equity.intro: This isn't a judgment about you. It helps us support you better based on where and how you live, so no one gets lost in follow-up.
equity.note: We take this into account to make access to health more fair, not to label.

zarit.title: How are you doing? (caregiver burden)
zarit.intro: This test is PRIVATE, just for you: it is not reported to anyone and stays on this device. It's 12 short questions about how you feel while caring.

hint.poca: You're doing well. Keep taking care of yourself and ask for support when you need it.
hint.moderada: It's a good idea to seek more support and moments of rest for yourself.
hint.alta: It's important to ask for help now. Talk to the health team or a trusted person.

items.time: Do you feel you have no time for yourself because of caregiving?
items.stress: Do you feel stressed between caregiving and your other responsibilities?
items.health: Do you feel your health has been affected by caregiving?
items.control: Do you feel you have lost control of your life?
items.distress: Are you distressed about the future of the person you care for?
items.social: Do you feel your social life has shrunk?
items.uncomfortable: Are you uncomfortable with some of the person's behaviors?
items.dependent: Do you feel the person depends heavily on you?
items.tense: Do you feel tense when you are with the person?
items.more: Do you feel you won't be able to keep caring much longer?
items.privacy: Do you feel you have lost privacy or intimacy?
items.overall: Overall, do you feel very overburdened?

paciente.title: After the diagnosis
paciente.intro: A diagnosis doesn't define who you are. You can live well, with support and planning.
paciente.note: Guidance only. Your health team supports you every step of the way.

0.b: Dementia progresses differently in each person. There are stages, and each one has its own supports.

1.b: Physical activity, connections, good sleep, keeping blood pressure and blood sugar in check, and keeping the mind active.

2.b: Decisions about health, finances and wishes while you can still express them. It brings peace of mind.

3.b: You have the right to clear information, to take part in decisions, and to be treated with dignity (Law 26.529).

4.b: Look for groups, associations and your health team. Asking for help is part of care.

dice.title: DICE method — for challenging behaviors
dice.intro: Behaviors are communication. DICE helps you understand and respond without conflict.

d.b: What exactly happened? When, where, and who was there.
d.ej: E.g.: “At 6 p.m., in the kitchen, they started looking for the key and got angry.”

i.b: What might have caused it? Pain, hunger, fear, noise, medication, boredom.
i.ej: E.g.: were they hungry or in pain? were they tired? was there a lot of noise?

c.b: Try a plan: adjust the environment, the routine, or the way you communicate.
c.ej: E.g.: turn down the radio, offer a mate, and always keep the key in the same place.

e.b: Did it work? If not, go back to investigating and try something else.
e.ej: E.g.: try it for a week; if it happens again, change just one thing at a time.

conductas.title: Common behaviors (DICE applied)
conductas.intro: Tap each situation to see why it happens and what to do.
conductas.more: Don't see your situation? Open the full guide

help.title: I need help now
help.body: If there's a risk to the person or to you, seek help right away. For day-to-day support, the full caregiver's guide is always available (offline too).

consent.title: Before we begin
consent.intro: This tool guides you and helps make better referrals. It does not diagnose or replace a professional. To continue, please read and accept:

points.0: We ask only what's needed. No real name — use initials or a nickname.
points.1: Everything is stored on this device. You decide whether to share it.
points.2: The result is an estimate, not a diagnosis.
points.3: You can view, export or delete your data whenever you want.

consent.note: By accepting, you start the screening. You can review your data in 'My data'.

datos.title: My data
datos.intro: Your data lives on this device. Here you're in control.
datos.count: You have {{n}} screening(s) saved on this device.
datos.confirm: Are you sure? This can't be undone.
datos.deleted: Done: your data has been deleted from this device.

points.0: Nothing is sent to a server without your consent.
points.1: The export is a file of yours, to share with whoever you want.
points.2: Deleting here removes the information from this device.

datos.importDesc: If someone shared their file with you (the KaizenAI “envelope”), load it here to add it to this device.
datos.simpleDesc: Larger text and screens without jargon.

community.title: Community
community.intro: This network is co-designed with the people who use it. Your voice changes what we build.
community.suggestTitle: What the community is asking for

seed.0: That the screening have audio for people who read little.
seed.1: More materials for rural caregivers, offline.
seed.2: A friendly reminder for blood pressure checks.

0.done: Done: A− / A+ control across the whole app.

1.req: Requested: that it work without internet.

2.req: Requested: that it not diagnose, only guide.
2.done: Done: visible notice on every result.

0.desc: Brings the perspective of someone living it.

1.desc: Carries the voice of everyday care.

seguimiento.title: Follow-up (my people)
seguimiento.intro: Who's up to date and who hasn't come back. Early re-contact prevents dropout.
seguimiento.msg: Hi {{alias}}, I'm from the brain health team. How are you doing? We want to support you.
seguimiento.msgCuidador: Hi, I'm from the brain health team. We wanted to know how the support is going.

import.err: Couldn't read the file (is it a KaizenAI envelope?).

tablero.anon: Aggregated, anonymous data — no personal information.

mi.title: My result
mi.empty: You haven't done your self-assessment yet.
mi.historyNote: Assessing yourself again from time to time lets you see how your results change over time.

agenda.title: Visit schedule
agenda.intro: Who to go see: follow-ups coming due or people who didn't come back.
agenda.empty: No pending visits for now. All up to date!

equipo.title: Memory Unit team
equipo.intro: Internal views for the pre-consultation. In the MVP it is a unified profile; they are separated later on.

medicina.0: Review the flagged medication (anticholinergics, BZD, Beers).
medicina.1: Confirm red flags and rule out reversible causes (B12, TSH, depression).
medicina.2: Assess cardiometabolic burden and sensory deficits.
medicina.3: Define tests and plan according to triage priority.

enfermeria.0: Take vital signs and complete missing data.
enfermeria.1: Review adherence and polypharmacy with the person.
enfermeria.2: Educate on protective habits and support network.
enfermeria.3: Coordinate appointments and follow-up with the health agent.

neuropsico.0: Apply the battery according to priority (ACE-III or another).
neuropsico.1: Choose the norm according to education level (Bruno criterion).
neuropsico.3: Integrate results into the Unit's report.

alarmas.title: Alert network
alarmas.intro: The shared queue that falls to you as {{rol}}. Each signal has an owner and an action that closes it; when it closes, it leaves behind a piece of data.
alarmas.vacia: No open alerts. The network is up to date.
alarmas.microVictoria: One more data point, one less gap. That's how the network is built.

detalleTexto.aguda: A signal that can't wait — review within hours, not weeks.
detalleTexto.discordancia: The person and their caregiver see things differently.

brecha.note: The area has no way to measure this today. It's not the person's or the health worker's task: it goes up to the provincial registry as a request for resources.
brecha.accion: Goes up to the registry as a gap — it isn't asked of the person.

diada.title: This is what we'll do together
diada.intro: Your next steps, {{alias}}. One at a time, no rush.
diada.alDia: You're up to date. Nothing pending for now.
diada.derechos: You decide: you can view, download or delete your data, and pause the support whenever you want.

pasoPersona.mirarJuntos: A talk to look at everything together
pasoPersona.evaluarAnimo: A talk about how you're feeling

pasoPersonaSub.medir: Your local health team coordinates it. It's quick.
pasoPersonaSub.reContactar: Your companion wants to know how you are.
pasoPersonaSub.mirarJuntos: With your family and the team, without judging anyone.
pasoPersonaSub.evaluarAnimo: Sometimes mood weighs on memory. It's worth a look.
pasoPersonaSub.protocoloInmediato: Best not to let it slide.
pasoPersonaSub.completar: The health team asked you for this.

voz.hint: Tap here to turn off the voice

gate0.pregunta: Did their face suddenly droop, did they lose strength in an arm, or did their speech get slurred?
gate0.aclaracion: Even if it has already passed. Mark Yes if it happened today or recently.
gate0.urgenciaBody: Time matters. Call 107 now or take the person to the nearest emergency room.

puerta.saludo: Hello. Let's take care of your memory.
puerta.bienvenida: This is your space. We'll go slowly, one thing at a time.
puerta.empezarNota: It's just a few questions. You can stop and continue whenever you want.

proxima.demografia: Tell us who you are (age and schooling)
proxima.lancet: A few questions about your health
proxima.cqc: Tell us how your memory is doing
proxima.tadlq: Tell us how your day-to-day is going

puerta.listo: Ready for your appointment — save or share your profile

empezamos.sub: Your brain health is already underway.

vamosBien.sub: Every step helps your doctor take better care of you.

completo.sub: Your profile is alive: it updates at every visit.

puerta.pedidos: What the health team asked you for ({{n}})
puerta.pedidosSub: Small things to take better care of you.

persona.falta: You're a little short — continue when you can.

vitales.falta: Waiting for the nurse to enter them.

tests.falta: Entered by the neuropsychologist if needed.

puerta.beneficioGen: Helps take care of your brain health.

beneficio.sueno: Sleeping well takes care of your memory.
beneficio.habitos: What you eat and how you move takes care of your brain.
beneficio.fisica: Controlling blood pressure and blood sugar protects your memory.

panel.title: Request profile completion
panel.intro: Mark what you want completed for {{alias}}. You can choose several things.

demo.subtitle: One person, many perspectives. Enter as each actor to see the integrated network.
